title: Lightly Sweet Japanese Raspberry Wagashi
description: A traditional Japanese dessert featuring raspberry jam and a glassy jelly texture, often served as a comfort food when feeling unwell. This recipe incorporates anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients and is lightly sweetened.
instructions: In a small saucepan, whisk together the water, sugar, agar agar powder, and turmeric over low heat until the sugar and agar agar are fully dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the lemon juice and ginger.
Let the mixture cool slightly, until it reaches a temperature of 120 degrees Fahrenheit.
Whisk the raspberry jam in a separate bowl until it is smooth.
Slowly pour the cooled agar agar mixture into the raspberry jam, whisking constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
Pour the mixture into small wagashi molds or a 9x9 inch baking dish.
Refrigerate the mixture until it is set, at least 3 hours or overnight.
Once set, remove the wagashi from the molds or cut it into small squares if using a baking dish.
Serve the wagashi chilled, garnished with fresh raspberries if desired.
